sections.title: Outsourcing risk management framework for financial institutions
sections.summary: Financial institutions must establish comprehensive outsourcing policies before engaging third-party service providers. These policies must define core activities that cannot be outsourced, assess materiality of outsourced activities, evaluate risks, and include contingency plans. The Board of Directors and senior management share responsibilities for oversight, including approving material outsourcing arrangements, reviewing risks annually, and ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements. The framework applies to all Bank of Mauritius-licensed financial institutions.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates specific risk management protocols for outsourcing that directly impact data transfer assessments by requiring financial institutions to evaluate third-party service providers, define material activities, and implement contingency plans for cross-border outsourcing arrangements.

requirements.text: Prior to outsourcing any activity, a financial institution should establish a comprehensive policy on outsourcing that guides assessment of whether and how an activity should be outsourced
The policy should include strategic goals, objectives, business needs, definition of activities that may be outsourced and core activities that cannot be outsourced
The policy should specify criteria for determining material outsourcing and processes for evaluating risks associated with outsourced activities
The policy should include criteria for evaluating outsourcing relationships with service providers, necessary controls and reporting processes
The policy should define limits on acceptable overall level of outsourced activities
The policy should establish eligibility criteria for selecting service providers considering any relations with the latter
The policy should address risk concentrations and risks from outsourcing multiple activities to the same service provider
The policy should include steps to ensure compliance with legal and regulatory requirements in both home and host countries
The policy should include a contingency plan in case of business disruptions
The Board of Directors must approve the outsourcing policy
The Board of Directors must assess outsourcing strategies and arrangements for consistency with strategic objectives
The Board of Directors must assess how outsourcing arrangements support financial institution's objectives and strategic plans
The Board of Directors must lay down appropriate approval authorities for outsourcing
The Board of Directors must approve material outsourcing arrangements
The Board of Directors must approve the exit mechanism for material outsourcing arrangements
The Board of Directors must assess management competencies for developing sound outsourcing risk management policies
The Board of Directors must review all material outsourcing activities and relevant reports at least annually
The Board of Directors must ensure continued maintenance of operational stability framework considering outsourced services
Senior management must evaluate risks and materiality of outsourcing activities
Senior management must implement sound outsourcing policies and procedures approved by the Board
Senior management must monitor and control all aspects of outsourcing arrangements on an ongoing basis
Senior management must keep the Board informed on material outsourcing risks in a timely manner
Senior management must ensure contingency plans including alternative service providers and resources for switching
Senior management must ensure internal audit and external auditors have authority to assess outsourced functions
Senior management must ensure regulatory and legal requirements are complied with at all times

sections.source_quote: Prior to the outsourcing of any activity, a financial institution should establish a comprehensive policy on outsourcing. The policy should guide the assessment of whether and how an activity should be outsourced.

sections.heading: (continued)
sections.title: Core functions that cannot be outsourced
sections.summary: [extraction failed: model output unparseable] Financial institutions cannot outsource core functions including board/senior management oversight, internal audit, and compliance. The Bank explicitly prohibits outsourcing these activities but allows case-by-case exceptions for intra-group outsourcing. Outsourced activities must be treated as material and meet the requirements of section 4.1.3.
sections.dtia_summary: Outsourcing core functions like internal audit and compliance is prohibited unless approved by the Bank as material intra-group outsourcing. This affects DTIA by requiring specific authorization and adherence to material outsourcing standards.

requirements.text: Financial institutions cannot outsource core functions including board/senior management oversight, internal audit, and compliance.
The Bank would not support the outsourcing of the abovementioned activities.
Exceptions for certain types of intra-group outsourcing may be allowed on a case-by-case basis.
Financial institutions that intend to outsource the abovementioned activities within the group must seek prior authorization of the Bank.
Outsourced activities must be treated as material outsourcing and meet the requirements of section 4.1.3.

sections.source_quote: Activities that are considered ‘core' and should not be outsourced are; board and senior management functions such as strategic oversight; internal audit function; and compliance function.
